daily_brief: The day's headlines are dominated by intensifying conflict across multiple theaters. In Lebanon, Israeli strikes killed at least 31 people, including children, as Prime Minister Netanyahu announced expanded ground operations despite an April ceasefire with Hezbollah [BBC, France 24, Le Monde]. Separately, Israel claimed to have killed Hamas military wing commander Mohammed Odeh in a Gaza City strike [BBC, Financial Times]. The US-Iran ceasefire, in place since April, faces new strains after Washington conducted strikes near Bandar Abbas that Tehran condemned as a 'gross violation' [BBC, The Hindu]. In the Democratic Republic of Congo, the WHO warned that Ebola is 'outpacing' response efforts, with suspected cases surpassing 900 and flights to Bunia suspended; attacks on health facilities have complicated containment [Guardian, Al Jazeera]. On the diplomatic front, Ukraine is urging the EU to appoint a mediator—with names like Angela Merkel and Mario Draghi floated—as US-led peace efforts stall and Russia escalates strikes on Kyiv [BBC]. Meanwhile, NASA unveiled contracts for lunar landers and rovers as part of its plan to establish a permanent moon base by 2032 [BBC, Globe and Mail].

top_stories.headline: Israeli Strikes Kill Dozens in Lebanon as Netanyahu Expands Ground Operations
bullets: Israeli airstrikes killed at least 31 people in southern Lebanon on Tuesday, including four children and three women, according to Lebanon's health ministry.
The Israeli military said it struck over 100 Hezbollah infrastructure sites in one of the heaviest bombardments since the mid-April ceasefire.
Prime Minister Netanyahu stated Israel is 'deepening' operations and 'fortifying the security zone' in Lebanon.
Hezbollah reported facing Israeli ground troops entering the town of Zawtar al-Sharqiyah; a strike near a public hospital in Nabatieh caused significant damage.

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Netanyahu's statements and the ceasefire's fragility.

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Focused on casualty details and hospital damage.

top_stories.headline: WHO Warns Ebola Outbreak in DRC Is 'Outpacing' Response Amid Attacks on Health Workers
bullets: The WHO Director-General said the Ebola outbreak in the DRC is outpacing response efforts, with 904 suspected cases and over 220 suspected deaths reported.
Attacks by residents on health facilities in Ituri province forced Ebola patients to flee; tents erected by Médecins Sans Frontières were burned.
The DRC suspended all flights to and from Bunia to contain the spread; Uganda has imposed border restrictions.
The Trump administration paused deportations to the DRC due to the outbreak, though at least one deportee remains stranded in Kinshasa.

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Detailed the attacks on health facilities and community mistrust.

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Focused on the isolation of Bunia and economic impact of flight suspensions.

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Article body unavailable; headline referenced community mistrust of medical workers.

top_stories.headline: Iran Condemns US Strikes Near Strait of Hormuz as 'Gross Violation' of Ceasefire
bullets: The US conducted strikes on Iranian missile sites and boats near Bandar Abbas on Monday, which Centcom described as 'self-defense strikes.'
Iran's foreign ministry condemned the action as a breach of the April ceasefire and warned of retaliation.
Iran's Revolutionary Guards claimed to have downed a US drone and fired at an F-35 fighter jet.
The strikes raise questions about the durability of ongoing ceasefire negotiations; the Financial Times reported ECB warnings that the conflict risks triggering a financial crisis.

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Presented both US and Iranian statements neutrally.

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Pakistan's mediation role and China's supportive stance.

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Headline focused on economic risks; article paywalled.

top_stories.headline: Israel Says It Killed New Hamas Military Wing Commander in Gaza Strike
bullets: Israel announced it killed Mohammed Odeh, the commander of Hamas's military wing, in a strike on a residential building in Gaza City on Tuesday.
At least three Palestinians were killed and dozens injured in the strike, which hit a busy market area ahead of Eid al-Adha.
Odeh had only recently assumed the role after his predecessor was killed in a similar strike days earlier.
Hamas has not commented; the strike occurred despite a ceasefire that began in October.

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Balanced account of Israeli claims and Palestinian casualties.

top_stories.headline: Ukraine Urges EU to Appoint Mediator as US Peace Efforts Stall
bullets: Ukraine's Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha called for 'more active participation by the European side' in peace negotiations with Russia.
Names floated for a potential EU envoy role include former German Chancellor Angela Merkel and former Italian PM Mario Draghi; neither has confirmed interest.
Russia has intensified strikes on Kyiv and threatened 'systematic' attacks, warning foreigners to leave the city.
EU foreign ministers are set to discuss re-engagement with Moscow at an informal meeting in Cyprus.

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Focused on diplomatic dynamics and potential envoy candidates.
